LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC.'S DEMURRER TO ALL FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF GAWFCO ENTERPRISES, INC.'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, COMMON COUNTS, AND FRAUD AND IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF BUS. & PROF. CODE 17200. GAWFCO'S REQUEST TO AMEND ITS SECTION 17200 CLAIMS COMPORTS WITH CALIFORNIA'S POLICY OF LIBERALLY ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS AND THUS IS GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-05-2018 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Continued To Nov-06-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Hussein Saffouri of te Ramsey Law Group, Tel. No. 1-925-284-2002 appearing
for the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Atty. Monica Monroe of Tucker Ellis LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-617-2210 appearing
for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Uber Technologies Inc.'s demurrer to all four causes of action in plaintiff Gawfco Enterprises, Inc.'s first amended complaint is overruled as to the first, second, and third causes of action for breach of contract, common counts, and fraud and is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for violation of Bus. & Prof. Code 17200. Liberally construed, the FAC sufficiently alleges breach of an oral contract and common counts by pleading the legal effect of the relevant terms of the oral agreement and Uber's conduct breaching that agreement: Uber's promise to pay with debit cards and replace them if they expired in exchange for the right to place promotional materials at Gawfso's gas stations and Uber's failure to replace the expired cards. Gawfco has also alleged with the requisite particularity its fraud cause of action by alleging what misrepresentations were made, by whom, when, and how. The failure to include the full names of Uber employees making the representations and to allege their authority to speak for Uber is not fatal to the claim. (Miles v. Deutsche Bank National Trust Co. (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 394, 403 (Reversing trial court order sustaining demurrer to a fraud claim where "The primary omission in the allegations is that plaintiff does not identify the names of the people he spoke to nor their authority to speak. In our view, this omission falls comfortably in the realm of information that lies more in the possession of defendants.")). Gawfco's request to amend its section 17200 claims comports with California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings and thus is granted. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
